messages.content:
Hi, I'm Walter Reid. I've started a new business around AI consultancy — well, technically I do a blend of AI, product management and technology, but I try to offer that service and move to AI full time when possible. The company is GG Solutions. I work with clients mainly in New York City, but I live in Greenwich, Connecticut. It's mostly online — no storefront or anything, unless you count my house as a place I work.

Honestly, nobody really knows about me. Because AI consultancy is so new and EVERYONE thinks they can do it themselves, I find it very difficult to stand out. I'm competing against people who watched a few YouTube videos and now call themselves AI consultants. The biggest thing I have going for me is that my AI prompts are different — when I talk to an AI, I probably get 70% of the effort compared to what most people get. I know how to treat nuance as a feature, not noise. But I don't know how to communicate that to people who don't even know they need me yet.

Walter, before we talk about how to stand out, I need to understand what you're currently doing to get in front of potential clients.

Right now — in the last month — how did your current clients find you? Was it a referral, did they search for you, did you pitch them cold, or something else?

And separately: are you spending any time or money right now on getting visibility, or is this still entirely word-of-mouth?
